create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'archived');
create type application_status as enum (
  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed'
);
create type submission_status as enum ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected');
create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
-- Which Apify actor run produced this snapshot. TikTok has one mode; IG has two (details + posts).
create type scrape_mode as enum ('tiktok_profile', 'ig_details', 'ig_posts');
create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
create type condition_metric as enum (
  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
  'post_must_tag_account'
);
create type notification_kind as enum (
  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
  'listing_version_changed', 'metrics_refresh_failed'
);
```

### 4.3 Denormalization

- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` — denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
- `listings.current_version_id` — FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
- `listings.approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count` — trigger-maintained counters.
- `applications.listing_version_id` — makes cascade a single UPDATE.
- `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` — generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed for feed filter.

### 4.4 RLS Model

Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```
RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.

### 4.5 Indexes

- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.

### 4.6 Triggers / Functions

All in `app_private` schema (security definer).

| Function | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` — if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` — updates `creator_profiles`. |
| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status → `approved` — increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |

### 4.7 Full Migration SQL

```sql
-- =========================================================
-- 0. Extensions
-- =========================================================
create extension if not exists "pgcrypto";
create extension if not exists "pg_cron";
create extension if not exists "citext";

create schema if not exists app_private;
revoke all on schema app_private from anon, authenticated;

-- =========================================================
-- 1. Enums  (see §4.2)
-- =========================================================
-- ... (as above)

-- =========================================================
-- 2. users
-- =========================================================
create table public.users (
  id         uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  role       user_role not null,
  username   citext not null unique,
  email      citext unique,              -- nullable for creators
  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  deleted_at timestamptz
);
create index on public.users (role) where deleted_at is null;

-- =========================================================
-- 3. creator_profiles (denormalized)
-- =========================================================
create table public.creator_profiles (
  user_id                     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  display_name                text,
  bio                         text,
  country                     text,
  tiktok_follower_count       integer,
  tiktok_avg_views_last_10    integer,
  tiktok_total_likes          bigint,
  tiktok_video_count          integer,
  tiktok_is_verified          boolean,
  instagram_follower_count    integer,
  instagram_avg_views_last_10 integer,
  instagram_media_count       integer,
  metrics_fetched_at          timestamptz,
  created_at                  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                  timestamptz not null default now()
);
create index on public.creator_profiles (tiktok_follower_count);
create index on public.creator_profiles (instagram_follower_count);

-- =========================================================
-- 4. lister_profiles
-- =========================================================
create table public.lister_profiles (
  user_id     uuid primary key references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  org_name    text not null,
  website_url text,
  logo_path   text,
  description text,
  created_at  timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at  timestamptz not null default now()
);

-- =========================================================
-- 5. social_links   (no OAuth fields — Apify-based)
-- =========================================================
create table public.social_links (
  id                      uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id                 uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
  platform                platform not null,
  handle                  citext not null,
  status                  social_link_status not null default 'linked',
  last_scrape_run_id      text,                      -- Apify run id
  last_scrape_attempt_at  timestamptz,
  last_scrape_error       text,
  fail_count              smallint not null default 0,
  -- Soft self-confirmation: set when the creator taps "Yes, that's me" on the
  -- first-scrape confirmation card (design §5.6). Used to gate the card's
  -- visibility. Reset to null on (un)link cycles so the card re-appears for
  -- the re-linked handle. fail_count is also reset to 0 on any fresh snapshot.
  handle_confirmed_at     timestamptz,
  created_at              timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at              timestamptz not null default now()
  -- Active-only uniqueness: unlinked rows release both their `(platform, handle)`
  -- reservation AND the `(user_id, platform)` slot so the same user can relink
  -- (same or different handle) without tripping UNIQUE.
);
create unique index social_links_user_platform_uniq
  on public.social_links (user_id, platform) where status <> 'unlinked';
create unique index social_links_platform_handle_uniq
  on public.social_links (platform, handle) where status <> 'unlinked';

-- History of unlinked rows is retained for audit; an admin override can claim a
-- handle currently held by an unlinked row (no extra logic required — the partial
-- index permits it).

-- =========================================================
-- 6. metric_snapshots (historized, one per Apify run)
-- =========================================================
-- TikTok: 1 row per refresh (scrape_mode='tiktok_profile' — contains followers + avg views).
-- Instagram: 2 rows per refresh (scrape_mode='ig_details' for followers, scrape_mode='ig_posts' for avg views).
-- The denorm trigger updates only the columns owned by each scrape_mode.
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
  apify_run_id      text,                  -- dedup across webhook retries
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
  following_count   integer,               -- populated on tiktok_profile + ig_details
  total_likes       bigint,                -- tiktok_profile only
  video_count       integer,               -- tiktok_profile (authorMeta.video) / ig_details (postsCount)
  avg_views_last_10 integer,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_posts
  is_verified       boolean,               -- tiktok_profile + ig_details
  raw_payload       jsonb,                 -- full Apify dataset item(s) for forensics
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default false,   -- promoted by trigger under lock
  error_message     text
  -- scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform coherence enforced by trg_metric_snapshots_coherence below.
);
create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);
-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode). So IG has at most two latest rows.
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans for rows stuck in 'refreshing' past the
-- 10-min timeout. Partial index keeps this sub-millisecond even with millions of rows.
create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';

-- =========================================================
-- 7. listings + versioning
-- =========================================================
create table public.listings (
  id                                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  lister_id                         uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete restrict,
  status                            listing_status not null default 'draft',
  title                             text not null,
  description                       text,
  -- Feed taxonomy. Enum-ish but stored as text for MVP flexibility; canonical list
  -- enforced at edge-function layer against `app_private.listing_categories` view.
  -- Used by the feed filter, by design §5.3's "Find a similar bounty" CTA, and by
  -- the creator notify-me-when-live toggle (§5.5). v1: 'beauty','fashion','fitness',
  -- 'food','tech','gaming','lifestyle','travel','finance','education','music','other'.
  category                          text not null default 'other',
  price_cents                       integer not null check (price_cents >= 0),
  currency                          text not null default 'USD',
  max_submissions                   integer,
  approved_submissions_count        integer not null default 0,
  active_pending_applications_count integer not null default 0,
  end_date                          timestamptz,
  current_version_id                uuid,
  version_number                    integer not null default 1,
  -- Cached thresholds for O(log n) feed filtering. Trigger-maintained by
  -- app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds(listing_id) whenever the current
  -- listing_version's pre-condition rows change.
  min_followers_tiktok              integer,
  min_followers_instagram           integer,
  -- Ephemeral cue consumed by trg_bump_listing_version; NULL in steady state.
  -- Values: 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'. Scalar edits (price/max_submissions/currency)
  -- are detected directly in the trigger from old.* vs new.*.
  version_bump_reason               text,
  created_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at                        timestamptz not null default now(),
  published_at                      timestamptz,
  closed_at                         timestamptz
);
create index listings_feed_idx
  on public.listings (status, created_at desc) where status = 'active';
create index listings_lister_idx on public.listings (lister_id, status);
create index listings_tt_threshold_idx on public.listings (min_followers_tiktok) where status = 'active';

-- Feed "find a similar bounty" filter (design §5.3): category + price band.
create index listings_category_price_idx
  on public.listings (category, price_cents) where status = 'active';

create table public.listing_versions (
  id                  uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_id          uuid not null references public.listings(id) on delete cascade,
  version_number      integer not null,
  price_cents         integer not null,
  currency            text not null,
  max_submissions     integer,
  snapshot            jsonb not null,
  previous_version_id uuid references public.listing_versions(id),  -- for diff rendering
  changed_fields      text[] not null default '{}',                 -- e.g. {price_cents, conditions}
  created_at          timestamptz not null default now(),
  unique (listing_id, version_number)
);
create index on public.listing_versions (listing_id, version_number desc);
alter table public.listings
  add constraint listings_current_version_fk
  foreign key (current_version_id) references public.listing_versions(id);

-- =========================================================
-- 8. listing_conditions (data-driven)
-- =========================================================
create table public.listing_conditions (
  id                 uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete cascade,
  kind               condition_kind not null,
  metric             condition_metric not null,
  platform           platform,

create table public.applications (

  listing_version_id uuid not null references public.listing_versions(id) on delete restrict,

  on public.applications (listing_id, creator_id)

-- =========================================================
-- 11. submissions + videos
-- =========================================================
create table public.submissions (
  id                   uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  application_id       uuid not null references public.applications(id) on delete restrict,
  status               submission_status not null default 'pending',
  cover_note           text,
  created_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  updated_at           timestamptz not null default now(),
  decided_at           timestamptz,
  decision_note        text,
  -- Override audit trail. Populated only when a lister approves a submission
  -- that has at least one failed post-condition (design §4.6 OverrideEligibilityDialog).
  -- override_by_user_id is the lister's user_id (redundant with applications→listings.lister_id
  -- in the common case but explicit here so a later "team accounts" v2 that permits
  -- delegated review doesn't need a schema change).
  -- override_reason is a free-text field typed by the lister; null for normal approvals.
  override_by_user_id  uuid references public.users(id) on delete set null,
  override_reason      text,
  constraint submissions_override_requires_approved

);

  on public.submissions (application_id)

create table public.submission_videos (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  submission_id     uuid not null references public.submissions(id) on delete cascade,
  platform          platform not null,
  url               text not null,
  external_id       text,
  oembed_cached     jsonb,

-- "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip (the ReuseBadge in design §4.6).
-- Aggregates how many other
-- submissions by the same creator use the same video URL (normalized by
-- external_id, not raw URL, so `?t=30s` variants collapse). The chip is only
-- shown to listers with RLS on submissions so they see their own submission_id

    on other.id = other_sv.submission_id
   and other.id <> s.id
  group by s.id;

1100	-- =========================================================
1101	-- 15. Triggers
1102	-- =========================================================
1103	-- Core bump routine — called directly (from listings UPDATE trigger) or via the
1104	-- edge function PATCH /listings/:id after it has applied listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1105	-- The edge function is the sole writer to listing_conditions + sample_videos once a listing is active;
1106	-- it calls app_private.request_listing_version_bump(listing_id, reason, changed_fields) AFTER
1107	-- those child edits commit, which performs an UPDATE listings SET ... that fires this trigger.
1108	create or replace function app_private.bump_listing_version()
1109	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1110	declare
1111	  versioned_changed boolean;
1112	  new_version_id    uuid;
1113	  prev_version_id   uuid;
1114	  changed_fields    text[] := array[]::text[];
1115	begin
1116	  if old.price_cents     is distinct from new.price_cents     then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'price_cents'; end if;
1117	  if old.currency        is distinct from new.currency        then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'currency'; end if;
1118	  if old.max_submissions is distinct from new.max_submissions then changed_fields := changed_fields || 'max_submissions'; end if;
1119	  -- The edge function passes a cue via the dummy `version_bump_reason` text column (set-and-reset in same UPDATE)
1120	  -- to indicate listing_conditions or sample_videos were edited:
1121	  if new.version_bump_reason is not null then
1122	    changed_fields := changed_fields || new.version_bump_reason;  -- e.g. 'conditions' | 'sample_videos'
1123	    new.version_bump_reason := null;  -- consume the cue
1124	  end if;
1125	
1126	  versioned_changed := array_length(changed_fields, 1) > 0;
1127	  if versioned_changed and new.status = 'active' then
1128	    prev_version_id      := old.current_version_id;
1129	    new.version_number   := old.version_number + 1;
1130	    insert into public.listing_versions(listing_id, version_number, price_cents, currency, max_submissions, snapshot, previous_version_id, changed_fields)
1131	      values (new.id, new.version_number, new.price_cents, new.currency, new.max_submissions,
1132	              to_jsonb(new), prev_version_id, changed_fields)
1133	      returning id into new_version_id;
1134	    new.current_version_id := new_version_id;
1135	
1136	    -- Capture affected creators BEFORE we update their status (so we can notify only them).
1137	    with cascaded as (
1138	      update public.applications
1139	         set status = 'cancelled_listing_edit', updated_at = now()
1140	       where listing_id = new.id and status = 'pending'
1141	       returning creator_id
1142	    )
1143	    insert into public.notifications(user_id, kind, payload)
1144	      select c.creator_id, 'listing_version_changed',
1145	             jsonb_build_object(
1146	               'listing_id',          new.id,
1147	               'new_version',         new.version_number,
1148	               'previous_version_id', prev_version_id,
1149	               'new_version_id',      new_version_id,
1150	               'changed_fields',      to_jsonb(changed_fields)
1151	             )
1152	        from cascaded c;
1153	  end if;
1154	  return new;
1155	end $$;

1161	-- Helper the edge function calls after committing listing_conditions/sample_videos edits.
1162	-- Produces an UPDATE that fires trg_bump_listing_version with the appropriate cue.
1163	--
1164	-- ORDERING CONTRACT (must be followed by the edge function):
1165	--   The edge function owns the full edit txn. Inside ONE transaction it must:
1166	--     1. `select id from public.listings where id = :lid for update;` — serialize against POST /applications and concurrent edits.
1167	--     2. `insert into public.listing_versions (...) returning id into v_new;`
1168	--        (new version row with empty conditions/sample_videos initially)
1169	--     3. `insert into public.listing_conditions (listing_version_id=v_new, ...)` for each pre/post-condition.
1170	--     4. `insert into public.sample_videos (listing_version_id=v_new, ...)` for each sample.
1171	--     5. `select app_private.request_listing_version_bump(:lid, :reason);`
1172	--        This swaps `listings.current_version_id` to v_new atomically. The
1173	--        `trg_bump_listing_version` trigger then runs `refresh_listing_thresholds`
1174	--        against the now-populated v_new, cascades applications, and emits
1175	--        notifications. Because conditions were inserted in step 3 against v_new
1176	--        — which is NOT yet `current_version_id` until step 5 — the per-row
1177	--        `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on `listing_conditions` becomes
1178	--        a no-op (it filters `where listings.current_version_id = NEW.listing_version_id`),
1179	--        so thresholds are computed exactly once, against the full set, at step 5.
1180	--     6. commit.
1181	--
1182	--   Feed queries never observe a partial-conditions state: conditions are
1183	--   written to a version that is not current, then made current atomically.
1184	--
1185	--   The `refresh_listing_thresholds()` trigger on listing_conditions is therefore
1186	--   MUST-filter by `current_version_id = NEW.listing_version_id` and return
1187	--   early otherwise. (See §15 trigger definition — keep this invariant.)
1188	create or replace function app_private.request_listing_version_bump(
1189	  p_listing_id uuid, p_reason text
1190	) returns uuid
1191	  language plpgsql security definer as $$
1192	declare new_ver uuid;
1193	begin
1194	  if p_reason not in ('conditions', 'sample_videos') then
1195	    raise exception 'invalid version bump reason %', p_reason;
1196	  end if;
1197	  update public.listings
1198	     set version_bump_reason = p_reason,
1199	         updated_at = now()

1200	   where id = p_listing_id
1201	  returning current_version_id into new_ver;
1202	  return new_ver;
1203	end $$;

1207	create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
1208	returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
1209	declare p platform;
1210	begin
1211	  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
1212	  if p is null then
1213	    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
1214	  end if;
1215	  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
1216	  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
1217	    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
1218	  end if;

1226	-- Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization.
1227	-- Fired BEFORE INSERT so we hold a row-level lock on social_links via advisory lock
1228	-- for the (social_link, scrape_mode) pair. This ensures concurrent webhook redeliveries
1229	-- and manual refreshes cannot both set is_latest=true.
1230	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
1231	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1232	declare
1233	  sl     public.social_links%rowtype;
1234	  lock_key bigint;
1235	begin
1236	  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
1237	    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
1238	    new.is_latest := false;
1239	    return new;
1240	  end if;
1241	
1242	  -- Serialize per (social_link_id, scrape_mode). hashtext gives a stable bigint mapping.
1243	  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
1244	  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);
1245	
1246	  -- Demote any previous latest for this (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
1247	  update public.metric_snapshots
1248	     set is_latest = false
1249	   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
1250	     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
1251	     and is_latest;
1252	
1253	  -- Claim latest for this new row.
1254	  new.is_latest := true;
1255	
1256	  -- Denormalize into creator_profiles — only columns owned by this scrape_mode.
1257	  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
1258	
1259	  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
1260	    update public.creator_profiles
1261	       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
1262	           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
1263	           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
1264	           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
1265	           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
1266	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1267	           updated_at               = now()
1268	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1269	
1270	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
1271	    update public.creator_profiles
1272	       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
1273	           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
1274	           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1275	           updated_at               = now()
1276	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1277	
1278	  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
1279	    update public.creator_profiles
1280	       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
1281	           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
1282	           updated_at                  = now()
1283	     where user_id = sl.user_id;
1284	  end if;
1285	
1286	  return new;
1287	end $$;

1312	-- Keeps listings.active_pending_applications_count in sync. Drives design §2.5
1313	-- age-bucketed nudge banners and the lister Dashboard "N waiting" tile. The
1314	-- counter reflects applications currently in status='pending' only.
1315	--
1316	-- Tracked transitions (new → effect):
1317	--   INSERT pending                          → +1
1318	--   UPDATE pending → anything else          → -1
1319	--   UPDATE anything else → pending          → +1   (rare: withdraw-undo not in v1, but safe)
1320	--   DELETE where old.status='pending'       → -1
1321	create or replace function app_private.maintain_pending_apps_counter()
1322	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1323	declare
1324	  delta smallint := 0;
1325	  target uuid;
1326	begin
1327	  if tg_op = 'INSERT' then
1328	    if new.status = 'pending' then delta := 1; target := new.listing_id; end if;
1329	  elsif tg_op = 'DELETE' then
1330	    if old.status = 'pending' then delta := -1; target := old.listing_id; end if;
1331	  elsif tg_op = 'UPDATE' then
1332	    if new.status = 'pending' and old.status <> 'pending' then delta := 1;  target := new.listing_id; end if;
1333	    if old.status = 'pending' and new.status <> 'pending' then delta := -1; target := new.listing_id; end if;
1334	    -- listing_id is immutable on applications, so old.listing_id = new.listing_id.
1335	  end if;
1336	
1337	  if delta <> 0 then
1338	    update public.listings
1339	       set active_pending_applications_count = active_pending_applications_count + delta
1340	     where id = target;
1341	  end if;
1342	  return coalesce(new, old);
1343	end $$;

1349	-- =========================================================
1350	-- 15b. Listing threshold maintenance
1351	-- =========================================================
1352	-- Keeps listings.min_followers_tiktok/instagram in sync with the MAX pre-condition
1353	-- threshold across the listing's CURRENT version. Fired on any write to listing_conditions.
1354	create or replace function app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds()
1355	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1356	declare
1357	  v_listing_id       uuid;
1358	  v_current          uuid;
1359	  v_affected_version uuid;
1360	begin
1361	  v_affected_version := coalesce(new.listing_version_id, old.listing_version_id);
1362	  -- Resolve the listing_id from the condition's version (works for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE).
1363	  select l.id, l.current_version_id
1364	    into v_listing_id, v_current
1365	    from public.listings l
1366	    join public.listing_versions lv on lv.listing_id = l.id
1367	   where lv.id = v_affected_version;
1368	
1369	  if v_listing_id is null then
1370	    return coalesce(new, old);
1371	  end if;
1372	
1373	  -- Ordering-contract invariant: edge function inserts conditions against a
1374	  -- version that is NOT yet current; only request_listing_version_bump swaps
1375	  -- current_version_id. If the affected row belongs to a non-current version,
1376	  -- skip — thresholds will be recomputed by refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump
1377	  -- when the version swap happens, and at that point the full condition set is
1378	  -- already in place. This prevents partial-set reads during multi-condition edits.
1379	  if v_affected_version <> v_current then
1380	    return coalesce(new, old);
1381	  end if;
1382	
1383	  update public.listings
1384	     set min_followers_tiktok = (
1385	           select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1386	             from public.listing_conditions
1387	            where listing_version_id = v_current
1388	              and kind     = 'pre'
1389	              and metric   = 'min_followers'
1390	              and platform = 'tiktok'
1391	         ),
1392	         min_followers_instagram = (
1393	           select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1394	             from public.listing_conditions
1395	            where listing_version_id = v_current
1396	              and kind     = 'pre'
1397	              and metric   = 'min_followers'
1398	              and platform = 'instagram'
1399	         ),
1400	         updated_at = now()
1401	   where id = v_listing_id;
1402	
1403	  return coalesce(new, old);
1404	end $$;
1405	
1406	create trigger trg_listing_conditions_refresh_thresholds
1407	  after insert or update or delete on public.listing_conditions
1408	  for each row execute function app_private.refresh_listing_thresholds();
1409	
1410	-- When a listing's current_version_id changes (version bump), recompute thresholds
1411	-- against the new version's condition set.
1412	create or replace function app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump()
1413	returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
1414	begin
1415	  if new.current_version_id is distinct from old.current_version_id then
1416	    update public.listings
1417	       set min_followers_tiktok = (
1418	             select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1419	               from public.listing_conditions
1420	              where listing_version_id = new.current_version_id
1421	                and kind = 'pre' and metric = 'min_followers' and platform = 'tiktok'
1422	           ),
1423	           min_followers_instagram = (
1424	             select max(numeric_threshold)::integer
1425	               from public.listing_conditions
1426	              where listing_version_id = new.current_version_id
1427	                and kind = 'pre' and metric = 'min_followers' and platform = 'instagram'
1428	           )
1429	     where id = new.id;
1430	  end if;
1431	  return new;
1432	end $$;
1433	
1434	create trigger trg_listings_refresh_thresholds_on_bump
1435	  after update of current_version_id on public.listings
1436	  for each row execute function app_private.refresh_thresholds_on_version_bump();
1437	
1438	-- =========================================================
1439	-- 15c. Session revocation (JWT denylist)
1440	-- =========================================================
1441	create table public.revoked_sessions (
1442	  session_id uuid primary key,
1443	  user_id    uuid not null references public.users(id) on delete cascade,
1444	  revoked_at timestamptz not null default now(),
1445	  reason     text,
1446	  -- Set to the session's original `exp` claim so cleanup can safely prune
1447	  -- after the JWT is no longer presentable anyway.
1448	  expires_at timestamptz not null
1449	);
1450	create index on public.revoked_sessions (user_id);
1451	create index on public.revoked_sessions (expires_at);
1452	
1453	-- Cache semantics (each edge-function container instance):
1454	--   * In-memory Set<session_id> keyed by session_id, loaded from
1455	--     `select session_id from public.revoked_sessions where expires_at > now()`.
1456	--   * TTL: 5 minutes. (Was 15. Tightened because custom-auth revocation latency
1457	--     is security-material — 5 min is the upper bound on how long a stolen token
1458	--     can still be used after the user clicks "log out everywhere".)
1459	--   * LISTEN channel `revoked_sessions_invalidate` — a NOTIFY is emitted by a
1460	--     trigger on INSERT carrying the new session_id as payload; the container
1461	--     adds it to the Set immediately without a DB roundtrip.
1462	--   * Reconnect policy: whenever the LISTEN connection drops for ANY reason
1463	--     (deploy, network blip, Postgres restart, idle timeout), on successful
1464	--     reconnect the container MUST execute a full reload query — otherwise
1465	--     revocations that happened during the disconnect are lost until the next
1466	--     TTL expiry. `listen_reconnected` events are logged for ops visibility.
1467	--   * Cold start: load the full set synchronously before the container accepts
1468	--     its first request. A cold container accepting requests with a stale
1469	--     denylist is the worst case we're avoiding.
1470	--   * Query path: `if (denylist.has(jwt.session_id)) reject 401`. O(1).
1471	--
1472	-- A JWT presenting a `session_id` that is missing from the denylist but present
1473	-- in the table (e.g., TTL just expired and NOTIFY hasn't fired yet) falls back
1474	-- to a point-lookup on Postgres for `session_id IN :set-of-sids-from-this-request`.
1475	-- This covers the 5-min window without flooding the DB for every request.
1476	create or replace function app_private.notify_revoked_session()
1477	returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
1478	begin
1479	  perform pg_notify('revoked_sessions_invalidate', new.session_id::text);
1480	  return new;
1481	end $$;
1482	create trigger trg_revoked_sessions_notify
1483	  after insert on public.revoked_sessions
1484	  for each row execute function app_private.notify_revoked_session();
1485	
1486	-- =========================================================
1487	-- 15d. Signup abuse throttle (per-IP)
1488	-- =========================================================
1489	-- Rolling window of signup attempts keyed by IP hash. Edge function rejects the 6th
1490	-- attempt from the same IP within 1 hour. Cleaned up by cron.
1491	create table public.signup_attempts (
1492	  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
1493	  ip_hash       text not null,                 -- sha256 of client IP + salt
1494	  user_agent    text,
1495	  role_attempted user_role not null,
1496	  success       boolean not null,
1497	  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
1498	);
1499	create index on public.signup_attempts (ip_hash, created_at desc);
1500	
1501	-- =========================================================
1502	-- 16. Cron
1503	-- =========================================================
1504	-- Mark snapshots stale after 24h (surfaces the "Outdated" badge in UI)
1505	select cron.schedule('mark-metrics-stale', '0 * * * *', $$
1506	  update public.metric_snapshots
1507	     set status = 'stale'
1508	   where status = 'fresh' and fetched_at < now() - interval '24 hours' and is_latest;
1509	$$);
1510	
1511	-- Janitor for stuck `refreshing` snapshots: if the webhook was lost (payload signed
1512	-- with a rotated secret, endpoint outage during delivery, etc.) the snapshot sits
1513	-- `refreshing` forever. After 10 min we fail-close so the creator sees an error
1514	-- and manual refresh becomes available again.
1515	select cron.schedule('fail-stuck-refreshing', '*/5 * * * *', $$
1516	  update public.metric_snapshots
1517	     set status = 'failed',
1518	         error_message = coalesce(error_message, 'webhook_lost')
1519	   where status = 'refreshing' and fetched_at < now() - interval '10 minutes';
1520	$$);
1521	
1522	-- Notifications retention: drop read notifications older than 90 days.
1523	select cron.schedule('prune-read-notifications', '0 3 * * *', $$
1524	  delete from public.notifications
1525	   where read_at is not null and read_at < now() - interval '90 days';
1526	$$);
1527	
1528	-- Signup attempt log retention: drop rows > 7d.
1529	select cron.schedule('prune-signup-attempts', '0 3 * * *', $$
1530	  delete from public.signup_attempts where created_at < now() - interval '7 days';
1531	$$);
1532	
1533	-- Revoked-sessions retention: drop rows past their original JWT expiry (safe to forget).
1534	select cron.schedule('prune-expired-revocations', '0 3 * * *', $$
1535	  delete from public.revoked_sessions where expires_at < now() - interval '1 day';
1536	$$);
1537	
1538	-- Application/submission expiration crons: INTENTIONALLY NOT SCHEDULED — no auto-expire in v1.
1539	```

1543	## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet
1544	
1545	**Transport mix:**
1546	- **Direct PostgREST** for RLS-safe reads (feed browse, notifications inbox).
1547	- **Edge Functions** for (a) external Apify calls, (b) multi-table atomic writes, (c) business logic, (d) JWT issuance, (e) webhook receivers.
1548	- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.
1549	
1550	Role = custom-JWT `role` claim.

1552	### 5.1 Auth
1553	
1554	**`POST /auth/signup-creator`**
1555	```ts
1556	interface Req {
1557	  username: string;
1558	  tiktok_handle?: string;
1559	  instagram_handle?: string;        // at least one of the two
1560	  display_name?: string;
1561	  country?: string;
1562	}
1563	interface Res {
1564	  user_id: string;
1565	  jwt: string;
1566	  social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
1567	  initial_scrape_queued: boolean;
1568	}
1569	```
1570	**Transaction boundary:** the identity rows (`users`, `creator_profiles`, `social_links`, and pre-created `refreshing` `metric_snapshots` rows) commit in a single Postgres transaction. Apify runs are dispatched **after** the transaction commits — a dispatch failure does NOT roll back the account. The creator gets a working account, the `social_links` row, and a `refreshing` snapshot; the UI displays "Couldn't fetch metrics — we'll retry automatically" and the daily cron picks the link up within 24h. This is intentional: Apify outages shouldn't block signup.
1571	
1572	**Per-run outcomes after the commit** (handled inline in the edge function before returning):
1573	- If `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` returns with `status: 'SUCCEEDED'` and items → parse + UPDATE snapshot to `fresh` inline. Counts toward the response's `initial_scrape_queued = false` flag per-platform.
1574	- If it returns with `status: 'RUNNING'` (inside-wait timeout) → webhook completes later. `initial_scrape_queued = true`.
1575	- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1576	
1577	Signup is idempotent on `(username)` — a duplicate call returns `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` before any Apify dispatch.
1578	
1579	**Per-IP throttle:** the edge function computes `sha256(ip + SIGNUP_SALT)` and inserts a `signup_attempts` row. If ≥5 attempts from the same `ip_hash` occurred in the prior 60 minutes (regardless of success/failure), return `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` with `retry_after_sec`. Salt rotates quarterly.
1580	
1581	Errors: `400 MISSING_HANDLE`, `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1582	
1583	**`POST /auth/signup-lister`**
1584	```ts
1585	interface Req { username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
1586	interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
1587	```
1588	Same per-IP throttle as `signup-creator` (shared `signup_attempts` table).
1589	Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1590	
1591	**`POST /auth/signout`**
1592	```ts
1593	interface Req { session_id: string; }
1594	interface Res { ok: true; }
1595	```
1596	Inserts a row into `revoked_sessions` with `expires_at = <jwt exp claim>`. Edge middleware caches the revocation set in-memory (5-min TTL; `LISTEN revoked_sessions_invalidate` pushes inserts live, with force-reload on reconnect — see §15c comments). JWTs with `session_id` in the set are rejected with `401`. Max stale-revocation window = 5 min (TTL) worst-case after a container cold-start that misses a NOTIFY during the one-request reload gap; typical window is zero because NOTIFY is synchronous.

1624	```ts
1625	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1626	interface Res {
1627	  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
1628	  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
1629	  retry_after_sec?: number;
1630	}

1632	Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1633	
1634	| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1635	|---|---|---|
1636	| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
1637	| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1638	
1639	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1640	
1641	**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
1642	- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
1643	- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1644	- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1645	- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1646	  ```sql
1647	  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
1648	  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
1649	  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
1650	  -- Apify runs are queued.
1651	  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
1652	  ```
1653	  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.

1655	### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1656	
1657	**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
1658	```ts
1659	interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
1660	  eventType:
1661	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
1662	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
1663	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
1664	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
1665	  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
1666	  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
1667	  social_link_id: string;
1668	  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1669	  run_id: string;
1670	  status: string;
1671	  dataset_id: string;
1672	}
1673	interface Res { ok: true; }
1674	```
1675	Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1676	
1677	Flow:
1678	1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
1679	2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
1680	3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
1681	4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1682	5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
1683	   ```sql
1684	   update public.metric_snapshots
1685	      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
1686	          raw_payload   = :items,
1687	          error_message = :error_message,
1688	          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
1689	          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
1690	          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
1691	    where apify_run_id = :run_id
1692	      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
1693	   ```
1694	   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
1695	6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696	7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1697	
1698	**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)

1747	### 5.6 Applications
1748	
1749	**`POST /applications`**
1750	```ts
1751	interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
1752	interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
1753	```
1754	Server re-evaluates eligibility; notifies lister.
1755	Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`, `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.
1756	
1757	**Concurrency contract — closes the race with `bump_listing_version`.** Without row locks, an applicant's `INSERT applications` can interleave with a lister's `UPDATE listings` that triggers the version bump + cascade-cancel, leaving a fresh `pending` row pinned to an already-stale version. The insert txn must serialize on the listing row:
1758	
1759	```sql
1760	begin;
1761	-- 1. Pin the listing to this txn. Both POST /applications and the edge-function
1762	--    wrapper of bump_listing_version must take this lock. The bump trigger runs
1763	--    under the UPDATE's own row lock, so they are mutually exclusive.
1764	select id, status, current_version_id
1765	  from public.listings
1766	 where id = :listing_id
1767	 for update;
1768	
1769	-- 2. Abort if the listing moved out of 'active' in that window.
1770	-- 3. Re-run eligibility against the NOW-current version_id (not the one the
1771	--    client thought was current when it opened the confirm sheet).
1772	-- 4. Insert applications.listing_version_id = the locked current_version_id.
1773	commit;
1774	```
1775	
1776	If the lister's version bump commits first, the creator's txn reads the new `current_version_id` after acquiring the lock; the eligibility rules may now differ, so the edge function re-checks and returns `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` with the new version's diff, which the client surfaces as "Terms just changed — review before applying" on Campaign Detail. No silent commit against a stale version is possible.

Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `409 PLATFORM_ALREADY_LINKED`.

interface Res { ok: true; scrape_run_id: string; }
```
Side effects: update handle + trigger fresh scrape. Old snapshots retained.
Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`.

**`POST /social/unlink`**
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).

**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
```ts
interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
interface Res {
  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
  retry_after_sec?: number;
}
```
Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.

| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
|---|---|---|
| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |

Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.

**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
  ```sql
  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
  -- Apify runs are queued.
  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
  ```
  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.

### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver

**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
```ts
interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
  eventType:
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
  social_link_id: string;
  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
  run_id: string;
  status: string;
  dataset_id: string;
}
interface Res { ok: true; }
```
Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).

Flow:
1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
   ```sql
   update public.metric_snapshots
      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
          raw_payload   = :items,
          error_message = :error_message,
          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
    where apify_run_id = :run_id
      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
   ```
   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.

**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)

**`POST /applications`**
```ts
interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
```
Server re-evaluates eligibility; notifies lister.
Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`, `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.

**Concurrency contract — closes the race with `bump_listing_version`.** Without row locks, an applicant's `INSERT applications` can interleave with a lister's `UPDATE listings` that triggers the version bump + cascade-cancel, leaving a fresh `pending` row pinned to an already-stale version. The insert txn must serialize on the listing row:

```sql
begin;
-- 1. Pin the listing to this txn. Both POST /applications and the edge-function
--    wrapper of bump_listing_version must take this lock. The bump trigger runs
--    under the UPDATE's own row lock, so they are mutually exclusive.
select id, status, current_version_id
  from public.listings
 where id = :listing_id
 for update;

-- 2. Abort if the listing moved out of 'active' in that window.
-- 3. Re-run eligibility against the NOW-current version_id (not the one the
--    client thought was current when it opened the confirm sheet).
-- 4. Insert applications.listing_version_id = the locked current_version_id.
commit;
```

If the lister's version bump commits first, the creator's txn reads the new `current_version_id` after acquiring the lock; the eligibility rules may now differ, so the edge function re-checks and returns `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` with the new version's diff, which the client surfaces as "Terms just changed — review before applying" on Campaign Detail. No silent commit against a stale version is possible.

```ts

interface Condition { metric: Metric; platform: 'tiktok' | 'instagram'; operator: 'gte' | 'bool'; threshold: number | boolean; }

}

  where status = 'active'
    and (min_followers_tiktok is null or min_followers_tiktok <= $creator_tt_followers)
    and (min_followers_instagram is null or min_followers_instagram <= $creator_ig_followers)
  order by created_at desc
  limit 50;
  ```
- Complex conditions (avg views, video count) evaluated client-side after first-pass server filter.

---

## 7. Critical Workflow Sequence Diagrams

### 7.1 Creator Signup + Initial Apify Scrape

```
App           EdgeFn(signup-creator)       Apify API          Our Webhook Fn        DB
 │─POST /auth/signup-creator {username, tt, ig}──>│                                    │

 │                                                  │     profileSorting:'latest'} waitSecs:60─>│
 │                                                  │─actor('apify/instagram-scraper').call
 │                                                  │    {directUrls:[ig_url],
 │                                                  │     resultsType:'details'} waitSecs:60──>│
 │                                                  │─actor('apify/instagram-scraper').call
 │                                                  │    {directUrls:[ig_url],
 │                                                  │     resultsType:'posts',
 │                                                  │     resultsLimit:10} waitSecs:60────────>│
 │                                                  │<─{runs × 3 with datasetIds}────────│
 │                                                  │  (for runs that returned inside 60s,
 │                                                  │   parse + UPDATE snapshot fresh inline;
 │                                                  │   for runs still RUNNING, leave refreshing
 │                                                  │   + register webhook for completion)│
 │<─{jwt, user_id, initial_scrape_queued: true}─────│                                    │
 │                                                                                         │

 │                                                                                         │

 │                                                                                 │  parse items
 │                                                                                 │─UPDATE metric_snapshots fresh─>DB
 │                                                                                 │   (trg denorm → creator_profiles)
 │  Realtime push metric_snapshots INSERT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────│
 │  UI updates follower count + avg views                                                    │
```

### 7.2 Creator Apply Flow

```
App              Client cache            EdgeFn(applications)         DB
 │─tap Apply────────>│                                                  │
 │ client eligibility│                                                  │
 │─POST /applications────────────────────────>│                         │

 │                                              │─evaluate() server-side─────│
 │                                              │─INSERT applications────────>│
 │                                              │─INSERT notification(lister)>│
 │<─{application_id}────────────────────────────│                            │
 Realtime: lister sees INSERT on applications
```

### 7.3 Lister Edit → Cascade

```
Lister app    EdgeFn(listings PATCH)    DB (trigger)              Affected creators
 │─PATCH /listings/:id price──>│                                       │
 │                              │─UPDATE listings──────────────────────>│

                                                             Realtime push to each creator
```

### 7.4 Submit → Review

```
Creator app    EdgeFn(submissions)       oEmbed          DB              Lister app

```

### 7.5 Metrics Refresh (Creator-Triggered)

App     EdgeFn(metrics/refresh)   Rate limiter   Apify API       Webhook Fn     DB

1	# Marketify — UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression — bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability — hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance — critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6–8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills — not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
53	- Body text (`--ink` `#0F172A`): **17.8:1** — passes WCAG AAA.
54	- Secondary text (`--ink-70` `#475569`): **7.9:1** — passes AAA.
55	- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** — passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** — only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
56	- CTA orange (`--cta` `#F97316`) is a background color under black label text (17.8:1), so the button itself is AAA; the orange is never used as foreground text.
57	
58	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas → `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
59	
60	### 1.3 Font Pairing
61	
62	**"Startup Bold"** — `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
63	
64	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600–700 | 28 / 22 |
67	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
68	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73	
74	**Usage rules:**
75	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
76	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
77	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78	- Line-length: max 65 characters → content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79	
80	**Dynamic Type / system font scale.** Honor iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scale up to **130%** on all text — all sizes above scale proportionally via `allowFontScaling={true}` (RN default). **Cap at 130%** to protect Neubrutalism's fixed line-heights (1.15 display) and the 340px content-width cap, both of which break visually past that threshold (letters start touching; cards overflow gutters). Above 130% we clamp the scale factor in a top-level `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3` setting — the OS-level preference is still reflected up to the cap, just not beyond it. For users who need more, the v2 accessibility settings screen will expose a "Full size-up (may break layout)" escape hatch. Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed — they're UI chrome, not content.
81	
82	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83	
84	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85	```
86	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89	```
90	
91	**Timing presets:**
92	```
93	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
94	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
95	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
96	```
97	
98	**Motion rules:**
99	1. **Screen transitions** — stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100	2. **Press feedback** — every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
101	3. **Status change (pending → approved/rejected)** — StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 → 1.12 → 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, ±4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. **Deep-link arrivals** (user tapped the push notification and the Submission Detail screen is the first screen they land on) — the celebration anchor is the StatusPill inside the just-mounted screen, triggered 220ms after mount (after the screen-slide settles) so the burst reads as a response to the arrival, not a hanging-over-from-last-screen artifact. If the screen mounts with `status` already `approved` (i.e. the user arrived via deep link, not via an in-app realtime update), we still fire the celebration exactly once, gated by a `celebration_seen_for_submission_id` entry in MMKV so revisits don't re-trigger.
102	4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** — custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `✓` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
103	5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** — on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` → `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
104	6. **Eligibility check animation** — tap "Apply" → criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
105	7. **Tab bar** — active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
106	8. **FlatList entries** — on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 → 0, opacity 0 → 1). Skip for subsequent renders. **Skip on screen re-entry within the same session** — a `useFocusEffect`-tracked `hasMountedOnce` ref gates the stagger so re-entering Feed from a pushed detail screen (or switching tabs back) doesn't replay the animation. Pull-to-refresh does *not* re-stagger either; new rows fade in individually (`LinearTransition` via `react-native-reanimated`'s layout animation API, 180ms). Full app relaunches reset the ref — the stagger is a first-impression-per-session flourish, not a per-mount one.
107	
108	**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.
109	
110	**Reduced motion:** wrap spring helpers in `useReducedMotion()`. Replace springs with `withTiming(value, { duration: 0 })` or short 120ms fade. Confetti and shake become no-ops; color transitions remain.
111	
112	### 1.5 Component Personality
113	
114	| Attribute | Decision |
115	|---|---|
116	| Corners | Cards 12px, buttons 10px, pills 999px, inputs 10px, images 8px. |
117	| Borders | 2px solid `--ink` on every card, button, input, chip. |
118	| Shadow | `offset (3, 3), color: --ink, opacity: 1, radius: 0` — signature hard shadow. Pressed collapses to (0,0). |
119	| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |
120	| Iconography | **Lucide React Native** exclusively. 24×24 viewBox, 2px stroke. Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants — Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |
121	| Imagery | Campaign sample videos get 2px ink border + hard shadow. Thumbnail aspect 9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for avatars. |
122	| Empty-state illustrations | Geometric line drawings using primary/cta/ink only. No stock illustration libraries. |
123	| Haptics | `expo-haptics` — Light on press, Medium on status change, Success/Error on approve/reject arrivals. |
124	
125	---
126	
127	## 2. Information Architecture
128	
129	Legend: **[C]** creator only, **[L]** lister only, **[S]** shared.
130	
131	### 2.1 Auth / Setup
132	
133	| Screen | Route | Role | Purpose | Primary CTA |
134	|---|---|---|---|---|
135	| Welcome | `/welcome` | S | First-launch brand intro | "I'm a Creator" / "I'm a Company" |
136	| Role Select | `/onboarding/role` | S | Commit to role | "Continue" |
137	| Enter Handles (Creator) | `/onboarding/creator-handles` | C | Username + TikTok handle and/or Instagram handle (at least one) | "Continue" |
138	| Fetching Metrics | `/onboarding/metrics` | C | Skeleton while Apify scrapes the profile(s); resolves in ~15–60s or slides into the next screen with a "still working in the background" chip | (auto) |
139	| Company Profile | `/onboarding/company-profile` | L | Capture username, email, org name, logo | "Finish" |
140	
141	*(Note: creator onboarding is handle-based — no OAuth, no platform consent screens. Our Apify scrapers pull public profile data from the handle alone. Handles are unverified in v1; there is no "claim your account" flow. The app simply trusts the creator to enter their own handle.)*
142	
143	### 2.2 Creator Screens
144	
145	| Screen | Route | Purpose | Primary CTA |
146	|---|---|---|---|
147	| Campaign Feed | `/(creator)/feed` | Browse active campaigns with eligibility toggle | Tap card |
148	| Filters Sheet | `/(creator)/feed/filters` | Refine feed (price, platform, eligible-only) | "Apply" |
149	| Campaign Detail | `/(creator)/campaign/[id]` | Full campaign info + live eligibility | "Apply" (or disabled "Not eligible") |
150	| Apply Confirm | `/(creator)/campaign/[id]/apply` | Confirm application | "Send application" |
151	| My Applications | `/(creator)/applications` | Track applications | Tap row |
152	| Application Detail | `/(creator)/applications/[id]` | Specific application status | "Submit video" (if approved) |
153	| Submission Composer | `/(creator)/applications/[id]/submit` | Paste video URL | "Send for review" |
154	| My Submissions | `/(creator)/submissions` | Track submissions | Tap row |
155	| Submission Detail | `/(creator)/submissions/[id]` | Status, feedback, video | "Open video" |
156	| Profile | `/(creator)/profile` | Own profile, handles, metrics | "Refresh metrics" |
157	| Edit Handles | `/(creator)/profile/handles` | Add / update / remove a TikTok or Instagram handle. Removing a handle stops future scrapes but preserves snapshot history. | "Save" |
158	| Notifications | `/(creator)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
159	
160	### 2.3 Lister Screens
161	
162	| Screen | Route | Purpose | Primary CTA |
163	|---|---|---|---|
164	| Dashboard | `/(lister)/dashboard` | Stat tiles + recent activity | "Create campaign" (FAB) |
165	| Create Campaign — Steps 1–5 | `/(lister)/campaigns/new/*` | Wizard: basics → price → pre → post → samples → review | "Next" / "Publish" |
166	| My Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` | List active + inactive | Tap card |
167	| Campaign Detail (lister) | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]` | Manage: toggle active, edit, counts | "Edit" / Toggle |
168	| Edit Campaign | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit` | Modify fields (triggers cancel-warning) | "Save changes" |
169	| Applications Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` | Review applicants with metrics | Tap row |
170	| Application Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications/[appId]` | Approve/Reject | "Approve" / "Reject" |
171	| Submissions Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions` | Review final videos | Tap row |
172	| Submission Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions/[subId]` | Video + checklist + decide | "Approve" / "Reject" |
173	| Profile | `/(lister)/profile` | Company profile | "Edit" |
174	| Notifications | `/(lister)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
175	
176	### 2.4 Shared / System
177	
178	| Screen | Route | Role | Purpose |
179	|---|---|---|---|
180	| Status Detail (auto-cancel) | `/shared/status/cancelled/[id]` | C | Explains what changed when a listing got edited |
181	| Error | `/shared/error` | S | Generic recoverable error screen |
182	| Not Found | `/shared/404` | S | Dead-link fallback |
183	
184	### 2.5 Empty & Error States (inline)
185	
186	- Feed empty (no campaigns): "Nothing live right now." + "Notify me" toggle.
187	- Feed empty (no eligible): see §5.5.
188	- Applications empty: "Apply to your first campaign." → feed.
189	- Submissions empty: "No submissions yet. Get approved on an application to start filming."
190	- Lister dashboard empty: "Post your first bounty."
191	- Applications inbox empty (lister): "Waiting for creators to apply."
192	- Network error: hard-shadow card with "Can't reach servers" + "Try again".
193	
194	**Age-bucketed nudges for non-expiring inboxes.** Applications and submissions do not auto-expire, so an inbox is never "full" in the OS sense — but stale rows rot quietly. On inboxes where any open row is older than a threshold, the list prepends a soft-warning banner (not an empty state — the list is non-empty):
195	
196	| Inbox | Trigger | Banner copy |
197	|---|---|---|
198	| Lister Applications Inbox | any pending application `created_at > 7 days` | *"N applications pending > 7 days. Review or close the listing."* |
199	| Lister Submissions Inbox | any pending submission `created_at > 5 days` | *"N submissions waiting > 5 days. Creators are watching the clock."* |
200	| Creator Applications | any pending application `created_at > 14 days` and no activity | *"N applications no one's touched in 2 weeks. Find a similar bounty?"* → routes to Feed |
